chunks.text: Scott Shenker is Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley. His contact information includes 415 Soda Hall, phone 510-643-3043, and email shenker@icsi.berkeley.edu. He also has a research assistant named Damon Hinson (damonh@berkeley.edu). Shenker maintains personal homepage, publications, dissertations, and research support links.

Scott Shenker's biography reveals he is also a researcher at the International Computer Science Institute (ICSI). Originally studying theoretical physics, he switched to computer science. His research has spanned performance modeling, networking, game theory, economics, system design, and privacy mechanisms. He remains focused on Internet architecture and splits time between ICSI and UC Berkeley. He acknowledges collaborators for enduring his humor and writing style.

Scott Shenker earned a Ph.D. in Physics from University of Chicago in 1983 and a Sc.B. in Physics from Brown University in 1978. His research areas include Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), covering Internet Architecture, Software-Defined Networks, Datacenter Infrastructure, Large-Scale Distributed Systems, and Game Theory and Economics. He is associated with research centers like the International Computer Science Institute (ICSI) and Networked Systems Lab (NETSYS).

Selected publications by Scott Shenker include works from 2020 to 1987. Notable papers cover topics like far memory for job throughput (2020), Tachyon memory speed storage (2014), Discretized Streams for fault-tolerant streaming (2013), Shark for SQL analytics (2012), distributed algorithmic mechanism design (2002), scalable content-addressable networks (2001), integrated Internet services (1994), fair queueing algorithms (1989), and epidemic algorithms for databases (1987).

Scott Shenker has received numerous honors and awards, including the IEEE CS Women of ENIAC Computer Pioneer Award (2023), Fiat Lux Faculty Award (2022), IETI Distinguished Fellow (2020), National Academy of Sciences membership (2019), Berkeley Visionary Award (2017), Paris Kanellakis Award (2017), multiple ACM SIGCOMM Test of Time Paper Awards (2017, 2011, 2006), IEEE Internet Award (2006), ACM Fellow (2003), ACM SIGCOMM Award (2002), and IEEE Fellow (2000).
